title: Create logger module with error, warn, and info functions
description: Implement src/utils/logger.js exporting error(), warn(), and info() functions. Each accepts a required message string and optional context object, and writes to the appropriate console method (console.error, console.warn, console.log) with a formatted string: [ISO timestamp] [LEVEL] message [optional JSON context].
acceptance_criteria: logger.js exports error, warn, and info as named functions
Each function writes to the correct console method (error→console.error, warn→console.warn, info→console.log)
Output format is [ISO8601] [LEVEL] <message> with optional JSON-serialized context appended when provided
Calling any function without a context argument does not print 'undefined'

title: Write unit tests for logger module
description: Create src/utils/logger.test.js using Node's built-in test runner (node:test + node:assert/strict). Tests must cover all three log functions, timestamp format, context serialization, and the no-context edge case.
acceptance_criteria: warn() test asserts output goes to console.warn with [WARN] level
error() test asserts output goes to console.error with [ERROR] level
Context serialization test asserts JSON appears after the message
No-context test asserts 'undefined' does not appear in output
All tests pass when run with npm test

title: Wire test script in package.json
description: Add a 'test' script to package.json that runs all *.test.js files under src/ using Node's built-in test runner, so npm test exercises the full test suite including the new logger tests.
acceptance_criteria: Running npm test executes logger.test.js and any other test files under src/
npm test exits 0 when all tests pass
